calendar.matters:
Petition For Instructions And Order Compelling Report And Accounting
Off Calendar Oct-03-2016 Off Calendar

Petition For Instructions And Order Compelling Report And Accounting
Off Calendar Jul-25-2016 Continued To Oct-03-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mazen Salfiti for Catherine Kelly. Edward Koplowitz for (co-trustees) Gregory Herrera and Jeffrey Herrera who is present in court.

Mr. Salfiti reported that he filed their status conference statement but that it is not on the register yet, that the point his client is making is that she has not left the country, that she has been working and traveling abroad, that they agree to mediation, that they want an informal accounting, that mediation would be unproductive without it, that the contention that Ms. Kelly took papers from the condo are not true, that the inconsistencies come from the trustees, that they agreed to mediation but are stuck at the informal accounting and that the co-trustees say that they provided one but all that was received are statements. Mr. Koplowitz stated that there is no reason to be here today, that the court ordered mediation, that he told Mr. Salfiti to pick a mediator and a date, that the reason Ms. Kelly's share was put into a trust was because of her behavior, that $200,000.00 is all that is left, that her share is $50,000.00, that they are wasting money here, that they have provided an informal accounting and that mediation is to cut down on costs. The Court asked if a Motion to Compel is imminent. Mr. Koplowitz stated that it is a small estate and that she is using up the money. Mr. Salfiti staed that the order says that mediation is to be done by late September, that Ms. Kelly wants to attend and that she want to be able to go in there with information. Mr. Koplowitz stated that usually the court's designation is by party agreement, that Ms. Kelly needs to either attend or be in contempt and that they have been given a complete accounting. Mr. Salfiti stated that the deposition notice was by email and not mail as indicated, that they have asked for an informal accounting and that they need an order for such. The Court stated that it will not order an accounting, confirmed that September 26, 2016 is the deadline for mediation and that they should set any depositions or discovery around that date. Mr. Salfiti asked if the mediation deadline could be extended and the Court responded no. The Petition for instructions and order compelling report and accounting was continued to October 3, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Instructions And Order Compelling Report And Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Continued To Jul-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mazen Salfiti for Catherine Kelly. Edward Koplowitz for (Co-Trustees) Gregory Herrera and Jeffrey Herrera who is present.

Mr. Salfiti stated that he has the substitution of attorney and will be filing it. The Court asked if the parties are ready to mediate. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he is happy that Mr. Salfiti has stepped in, gave background information to the court, stated that the property was sold and Ms. Kelly received her share, that there is $200,000.00 left in the trust, that based on the share allotted to Ms. Kelly she is entitled to income only which comes to about $500.00 per year, that they sent a complete accounting to Mr. Salfiti, that it included a complete physical inventory, bank statements and tax documents, that Ms. Kelly lived in the property and took papers, that they cannot do an actual accounting without taking her deposition but she is out of the country until 2017 and that mediation will not work without her present. Mr. Salfiti stated that he just came into the case, that there was an agreement between all beneficiaries to sell the condo, that they asked for an informal accounting which was received and has been reviewed, that they are amenable to mediation, that there is distrust between the parties and that the accounting is not a complete accounting. The Court asked about setting a 90 day deadline to complete mediation and asked about taking the deposition via telephone. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he has done depositions via video feed but that it is very expensive. Mr. Salfiti stated that his client has been out of the country for 5-6 years, that he will try to get her available and that this case is ripe for mediation. Mr. Koplowitz suggested setting a status conference in 30 days and a mediation deadline for 90 days. Mr. Salfiti asked about the request for an accounting. The Court asked counsel if he has fully reviewed the accounting provided. Mr. Salfiti responded that the distrust is very deep in this case and that he thinks the accounting will be the way to clear things. Mr. Koplowitz stated that Ms. Kelly is non cooperative, that there are 3 other siblings, that there is a disconnect as to what is really there, that if they are unable to settle an accounting can be requested and that they need information from her to complete an accounting. The Court set September 26, 2016 as the deadline to mediate and asked about further steps. Mr. Koplowitz stated that if Ms. Kelly returns for the mediation then they can take her deposition. The Petition for Instructions and Order Compelling Report and Accounting was continued to July 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than September 26, 2016. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by September 19, 2016. The matter was continued to and the Parties are to return on July 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Boncato. (Not Reported)
